https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-calculate-the-time-difference-between-the-same-timestamp-if-less/qaq-p/13850887/comment-id/4881743#M4881743

entry.title: Quick hacks: Displaying fields
entry.content:
Like most developers, I'm very lazy and want to do things the neat, quick and cool way whenever I can. Today's quick hack is showing you how I very quickly display the fields of a data resource, whether it be on a list or on a detail page.

This hack is specifically for those text or number type fields that you don't want to style in any specific way, but want to just show with a label and the content below.

Example of fields with label and value

Whenever I need to do this, I take the Object I want to display and do a nifty formula using MAP, KEYS and LOOKUP. The following example is done in a situation like above when the Object I want to display is repeated.

 

MAP(KEYS(repeated.current), {key: item, value: LOOKUP(repeated.current, item)})

 

If you don't want all of the fields displayed (or want to display some fields in a different way), you can omit those fields using OMIT_KEYS formula, e.g.

MAP(KEYS(OMIT_KEYS(repeated.current, ["id", "image"])), {key: item, value: LOOKUP(repeated.current, item)})

In addition, you could of course for example style the field names (e.g. capitalizing with CAPITALIZE).

Hope this helps or gives you ideas for how to use formulas in your apps!

entry.title: Re: Move data only to first product in the hierarchy belo...
entry.content: Not sure how you define 'first product in the PL7 ', if you only maintain property of your first product in PL7, then you should not get value to other products.

entry.title: Planned Orders list of the last MRP run
entry.content:
Hello, experts.
In my scenario, MRP is scheduled in the background and runs once a day; after the MRP run, I just need the list of planned orders generated by the last MRP run.

The reason for this is that planners used to convert priority planned orders on the same day, while lower priority planned orders remained unchanged. So, after the MRP run, if I check the manage planned orders app, I'll see the lower priority planned orders as well.

In COOIS, when I run the report, I don't see any data in the created on column. Is there a way to meet the requirement.

entry.title: Re: 🔵 Integrating Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA with ISLM
entry.content: @Cocquerel, I'm unable to access the slides.

entry.title: Re: PaPM function doesn't get deployed at first, and only...
entry.content:
Hello Leonardo,

I understand that you are encountering this issue when executing PaPM transactions. To fix the issue, kindly follow the steps below:

1. Transaction SICF: Please make sure that node WDR_APC is activated
2. Web Dynpro application WD_GLOBAL_SETTING: Please make sure that the parameter WDNOTIFICATIONSERVICE is set to value PUSH. You can use this parameter also as URL Parameter.
3. SAP Note 2748620 - Requirements for Notification Service through WebSocket communication protocol

If still does not work, incident can be created and assign to Webdynpro component BC-WD-ABA-RUN.

Hope this helps!

Best regards,
Kevin

entry.title: Re: SAP Android SDK - offline odata - batch CUD operations
entry.content:
Hi @yohann_weberfr,

The SAP BTP SDK for Android provides specific classes for submitting batch requests to the backend. Check out following guide: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/features/backend-connectivity/android/using-odata-api.html#batch-processing 

Best regards,

Robin

entry.title: Accrual Process directly FI
entry.content: S4HANA Public Cloud product does not have Accrual Engine. In one of our projects, rent invoices will come from the RE module and I need to periodise it from the FI module. What can I do for this, do you have a suggestion? There are short-term and long-term differentiations in country localisation. In other words, I need to differentiate the amount found during the year and the amounts to be found in the coming years.

Best regards

Atakan

entry.title: Re: Internal behaviour of dump marker and rsync marker in...
entry.content: Hi Dilip, the internal behaviour with dump marker should be similar for MSA/WSB if I'm right. Regarding the data in queue, you can check this: "For each DSI that resumes with the skip to resync marker parameter, the DSI outbound queue records in the Replication Server system log that DSI has received the resync marker and also records that from that point forward, DSI rejects committed transactions until it receives the dump database marker." https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_REPLICATION_SERVER/62f3e3a6d49342cf9b7d72ee726a48b0/fd0bedadbd1c1014b1beabd7f2f84de7.html?locale=en-US&q=resync+marker

Rajendra Pandey

entry.content:
please help me with the hyperlink in ADOBE forms.

I have tried following methods but no luck.

entry.title: change the payment conditions
entry.content: Dear colleagues, I have a question about the relationship between a debtor (VD02) and a maintenance contract (VA42) in SAP. When I change the name of a debtor in VD02, this name also changes directly in existing maintenance contracts. But when I change the payment conditions of a debtor in VD02, for example from 0019 to 0313, the payment conditions do NOT change in the existing maintenance contracts. Can this be set up so that a change of payment conditions is adopted in all existing maintenance contracts for this debtor?

entry.title: Re: FocusBuild - TOCs not imported immediately
entry.content: Hi Shawn, thanks for your help. I took a look at transaction type SM1J in SM34->AIC_SETTINGS2 (there are no ZXXX transaction types in our systems). Under E004, the action is COPY_ALL_ENH. So TOC should be imported automatically. I will open a support ticket with SAP after getting some more information about the RFCs which show this behaviour.

entry.title: Re: FocusBuild - TOCs not imported immediately
entry.content: Hi Shawn, thanks for your help. I took a look at transaction type SM1J in SM34->AIC_SETTINGS2 (there are no ZXXX transaction types in our systems). Under E004, the action is COPY_ALL_ENH. So TOC should be imported automatically. I will open a support ticket with SAP after getting some more information about the RFCs which show this behaviour. Regards Andre

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP WORLD,
entry.content: enjoy the content 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant -...
entry.content:
Welcome Felicia!

One piece of advice from me (former consultant for S4 Sales in Public and prior to that in on-prem) is even though all that you'll need to obtain the certification is in the Learning Journey, I've found the book 'Configuring SAP ERP Sales & Distribution' invaluable through all my years working with sales. It shows the sample business scenarios that are relevant to the topic (eg. why do you need a proof of delivery at all?) and the configuration options in an on-prem system behind that. While some of that configuration isnt available to modify in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's still very useful to know the driving mechanic and what it does (e.g. item categories). Good luck!

entry.title: Lessons from the SAP Advisory Council for Waste & Recycling annual meeting
entry.content:
On September 25th  and 26th took place in Walldorf the annual presential meeting  of the SAP Advisory Council for Waste and Recycling is a,  by invitation only, a select group of companies  willing to collaborate in an open environment with SAP. With more of 25 editions, the Advisory Council is a well established forum whose members drive their companies digital vision and stablish a community beyond the physical meetings with the following purposes.

​- Feedback and validation

​- Collaboration

​- Communication

​- Product adoption information exchange

 ​Image:  SAP Advisory Council for Waste & Recycling meeting atendees

​This year edition attended more than 25 delegates from 9 companies plus  Prologa, in the condition of developer of strategic industry cloud solutions for the Waste and Recycling industry and Gruler & Cleve Consulting  which accepted the invitation to share their experiences and help conducting a design thinking workshop.

Agenda for this year was divided in three parts:  product strategy and development updates, ideation workshop on Artificial Intelligence and conversion experiences into S/4HANA.

​SAP  product strategy and development updates were presented by Solution Management and Product Management teams (Image:  Uwe Zachmann and Somik Sarkar, Waste industry responsibles from Product Management team) which offered their respective perspectives, this is from extending the scope of current solution into the recycling part of the value chain to a detailed description and demonstration of the new developments released for the SAP for Waste & Recycling  part of S/4HANA plus insights of the ongoing and future developments.  All these updates provided were explained in the context of the overall SAP product portfolio strategy around RISE offering, where Artificial Intelligence capabilities will play a key role.  In this sense, and as an introduction for the later comprehensive ideation session on this subject, a PoC of Jule showing a use case for the waste collection process was shown.

​Prologa presented very interested and relevant updates as well.  Of special relevance has been the introduction of Mr. Christoph Hillmann as Prologa VP for Circular Economy & CoDevelopment who made a vibrant presentation of their product strategy and the ongoing development roadmap for their components, with special focus in the Order Planning and Dispatching solution plus a spark of their updated go – to – market strategy which will include an event next november   

 

​Image:  Ideation session

​Second part of the meeting has been an ideation workshop on AI can help to develop waste & recycling business. Session was brillianty conducted by Isabel Creuznacher from Gruler & Cleve Consulting jointly with Henán Sendra from the Center of Expertise - CoE of SAP BTP.

​ Part one

  • Short Intro of objective WS
  • Inspiration overview:  AI for SAP
  • Intro Design Thinking as Method for WS

​Part two

  • Teambuilding
  • Exercise Needfinding Journey (participants):  discuss the journey and define the problems
  • Group presentations main pain points (participants)

 

​Part three

  • Split teams according to problem area
  • Intro Ideation Session
  • Ideation Sessions for use cases

oBrainstorming / “crazy 8” (individual)

oSelect for the best 10 ideas (group)

  • Selection of idea (ideation matrix for feasibility)
  • Wrap-up

​Third part of the meeting was dedicated to the conversion experiences of SAP ECC systems into S4HANA.  A very big thank you to our co-chairs Roman Aiyer from WBD - Duisburg and Matthias Reich from Prezero for the great feedback on their S4HANA conversion projects with the great help of Mr. Jürgen Krist.

​.We are confident that more successful and interesting sessions will come in the future. Noticing that members play an active part in the orchestration and conduction of activities is a positive signal.  For all involved SAP departments, the value of the Advisory Council is undisputed.

 

​

entry.title: Announcing the Water Resilience Conference: Cape Town, Southafrica, November 13th – 14th
entry.content:
​We are pleased to announce an insights session on innovative strategies and technologies to enhance water resilience and drive digital transformation in water and waste water services companies in Africa. This session is in collaboration with our global alliance partner EY, which brings leading knowledge, experience and global expertise in this sector

​Water resilience, this is the ability of water and wastewater companies to support and mitigate the effects of disasters, infrastructure challenges, population growth and the growing number of unpredictable challenges mainly due to climate change. Companies are required to implement strategies and technologies that ensure a continuous and reliable supply of clean water and secure a healthy enviroment, even in the face of disruptions.

​This includes the use of smart metering platforms, intelligent reporting and satellite imaging to monitor and manage water resources effectively. By building resilience, government/water companies can enhance their capacity to adapt to changing conditions, protect public health and support sustainable development.

 ​Source:  FARYS, PIDPA and De Watergroep: “Digitale Watermeter. The power of Smart Metering”. Utiliteis Conference, Seville, 2024

​Sessions are designed to bring  inspirational use cases and open a discusión among participants. Current confirmed tracks will be: 

  • City of Cape Town - How a Smart Metering Platform will ensure water resilience in the City of Cape Town.
  • SAP - : Building resilience in water companies; evolving to data-driven insights and decision-making using a smart water platform
  • FARYS - WaterUnie : How Intelligent Reporting ensures evidence-led decision making at a European water company
  • EY Africa and EY USA: How Satellite Imaging supports water resilience strategies
  • SAP - Smart Energy and Water Platform demonstration.  What does this mean to a water utility company? How to derive value from latest technology solutions
  • SAP - Developing a customer centric culture. What is the purpose, benefits and challenges. What is the impact on IT landscapes at water utilities?
  • EY Africa - : Government & Infrastructure topic – Asset Management as a discipline – what does good look like?
  • SAP - and EY Africa : Ageing Infrastructure – Intelligent asset management: how digital innovations are transforming field operations and rehabilitation investment strategies
  • FARYS - WaterUnie : Holistic approach for asset management: Funding/Investment, Field execution and Spatial integration

 ​Sessions will take place at EY Cape Town offices and Bellville CoCT Water Department. We are looking forward to share these insights with attendees while exploring and learning from their experiences in what promises to be an enlightening and engaging discussion. For any queries you may have please reach out to Hannes Venter hannes.venter@sap.com or myself.

​Looking forward to see you in Cape Town!

entry.title: Re: Internal behaviour of dump marker and rsync marker in...
entry.content:
Hi Tony,

My whole intention behind mentioning/specifying the work 'resync' is to tell that synchronizing the out of synchronized 'RDB' but not the method to use the option 'resync marker' in RepAgent and DSI to synchronize.

1. In WSB whenever we want to synchronize the 'RDB', we drop and recreate the connection. Once we do this "enable replication marker" will be written into PDB's transaction log. After this step when a dump is triggered a 'dump marker' will be written into the PDB's log which will then carry forward to the Replication Server System. The behaviour applies to MSA (Database Level)as well when a subscription is defined with dump marker?
2. Coming to my second question, if we don't purge(because we can't mark the RS into standalone) the OBQ of the effected RDB before we synchronize(just suspending the DSI) how the existing data in the OBQ of the effected RDB will be handled?

Regards,
Dilip Voora

entry.title: Re: SAP and Arm64 version of Windows 11 with Prism emulat...

in regards to ABAP development tools for Eclipse: Do you know our ADT installation guide? It is our official document. It contains a section with the requirements.

Does the document helps you?

Kind regards,

Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Embrace Defect Corrections with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello Thomas,

You can refer to the SAP Cloud ALM Road Map to see which features are planned for release with the timeline - https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800002513&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q4%202024
Road Map is being updated on a regular basis by Product Management. 

Stay curios! 🙂

entry.title: Delivery Address sequence
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In delivery address sequence we have Ordering party (order partner) and Goods recipient (order partner). Can you please let me know what does Ordering party (order partner) and Goods recipient (order partner) address refers to.

Regards,

Preethi

entry.title: Re: How to Set Up the Email Notification for Flexible Workflow in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello 

I've created a flexible workflow for WS02000029, I've copy the following email templated into my company name space.

/***/SD_SLS_CMR_APPROVAL_EMAIL

/***/SD_SLS_CMR_DEADLINE_EMAIL

/***/SD_SLS_CMR_REJECT_EMAIL_T

/***/SD_SLS_CMR_RELEASE_EMAIL_

/***/SD_SLS_CMR_REWORK_EMAIL_T

/***/02000029_COMPLETE_NEGATIV

/***/02000029_COMPLETE_POSITIV

/***/SWF_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS

When the requestor creates a credit memo, the email goes to the Approver as well as the deadline email that is /***/SD_SLS_CMR_APPROVAL_EMAIL and /***/SD_SLS_CMR_DEADLINE_EMAIL.

The problem is when the Approver Approves/Rejects or Reworks, theres no email sent out to the requestor.I've check SOST and theres no email in the queue, email addresses are maintained for the requestors in SU01.

The 2 decision tasks 02000054 and 02000055 are binded for WS02000029 in transaction PFTC.

There workflow works fine, its just the emails that dont get sent out.

  Is there some steps that I'm missing somewhere?

We are using S4/HANA 2023 on premise, SAP FIORI for S4HANA 2023

Thanks

Adhil

entry.title: 3rd Latin America Executive Value Network (EVN) for Water and Waste Water Services companies
entry.content:
Current edition of the Executive Value Network (EVN) pursues the consolidation to what is an regional knowledge sharing forum focused into the analysis and discussion of digital transformation use cases of Water and Waste Water companies with the purpose to get valuable insights from peers and explore how to improve inefficient traditional processes while embracing new relevant technologies. 

 Image: EVN previous meetings in Bucaramanga (2022) and Cartagena de Indias (2023)

​Success of previous years EVN editions have motivated to meet this year in Barranquilla, hosted by Triple A which is the local water company.  Latin America EVN also has inspired other territories of the region to explore digital transformation cases in the water sector and network among peers and last may  there was a similar event in Mexico, hosted by hosted by the Servicios de Agua y Drenaje de Monterrey (Mexico)  where you can find a summary here 

 

​Sessions are designed to bring  inspirational use cases to open a discusión among participants. For current edition tracks Will be

  • How an intelligent water management platform assure water and waste water services companies resilience to adress corrent and future challenges.
  • Developing a Customer first culture:  purpose, benefits and challenges. How its application impacts corporate IT landscape.
  • Technical asset Management as a discipline:  which are the benefits of efficiency? How Innovation are trasforming field work and investment strategies
  • How a data driven policy assures transparency, resource optimization and intelligent decisions

Sessions will be complemented with a site visit to Barranquilla's new Marllorquin Swamp Ecopark 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

​Thanks to our partners  Misait and Smart Energy and Water – SEW (links), the Executive Networking Dinner help attendees to join an executive community that extends beyond the conference, gaining knowledge from peers and other companies, partners and vendors.

​For those interested to join and take part in our discussions we have set up a registration website where updates on agenda and other useful information is as well  https://events.sap.com/lac/evn-agua-alcantarillado-lac-north-2024/es/home

​Looking forward to see you in Barranquilla!

entry.title: Re: RAP, create child entities based on static factory ac...
entry.content: Thank you @Kangkana. I will correct it . Can you also please check if Modify entities is a valid syntax. Since the scenario is to create child entity independent of Parent entity and I am getting error that create child entity is not possible.

entry.title: Re: Display hierarchies in BW4 for authorization relevant...
entry.content: Unfortunately this does not work as expected... Once the user is authorized for the root node, the user sees values for complete world. This is not what we want. He should still be able to see values for his company code only. Value and hierarchy authorizations work always in combination.

entry.title: Re: RAP, create child entities based on static factory ac...
entry.content: Thank you @Kangkana. I will correct it . Can you also please check if Modify entities is a valid syntax. Since the scenario is to create child entity independent of Parent entity and I am getting error that create child entity is not possible.

entry.title: Re: Infotype 0001An exception with the type CX_SY_DYN_CALL_ILLEGAL_TYPE occurred, but was neither ha
entry.content: It actually showing this error for some users. PA/PD integration is turned on PLOGI ORGAS. However, If I turn off PA/PD, the error doesn't appear. Exactly I am not able to catch the issue.

entry.title: Initial value of 0FISCPER is replaced wrongly
entry.content:
Hi, after upgrade to BW7.50 SP029 we are faced with the problem, that our exit-variable using initial query proposed value "#" is replaced to "000000". That leads to an error in our coding as it is not interpreted as initial value nor as "#" anymore.

Does anyone have an idea?

  METHOD bi_f_ce_za_0fiscper_006.
* Deklaration
    DATA:
      ls_var_range LIKE LINE OF it_var_range,
      ls_range     TYPE rrrangesid,
      lv_bool      TYPE i.

* Initialisierung
    CLEAR ls_var_range.
    CLEAR ls_range.

* Aus dem Intervall Periode/Geschäftsjahr die Differenz der Anzahl Perioden ermitteln
    READ TABLE it_var_range WITH KEY vnam = cv_bi_ma_i_o_0fiscper_001 INTO ls_var_range.

    IF ls_var_range-low eq '#'
    OR ls_var_range-low IS INITIAL.
      lv_bool = 0.
    ELSE.
      lv_bool = 1.
    ENDIF.

    CLEAR ls_range.
    ls_range-sign  = 'I'.
    ls_range-opt  = 'EQ'.

    ls_range-low = lv_bool.

    APPEND ls_range TO et_range.

  ENDMETHOD.

 

Regards,

Peter

entry.title: Re: Sync & Use SWIFT Code (Bank Data) from SAP ECC to SAP Ariba-SLP via WebServices and vice ver
entry.content:
I am facing a similar issue. The standard mapping proposed for SWIFT Code is vendor.bankInfos.bankAccountExternalID but this is incorrect.

Mapping the SWIFT code to this field will prevent registering a second bank account with the same bank (and same SWIFT Code) in the supplier profile in SAP S/4.

Can anyone suggest the correct mapping for SWIFT Code?

 

Thanks

Smeeta

entry.title: How to add a post on the Supply Chain community
entry.content: Hi everyone, sorry new here and struggling to post a question on the Supply Chain community?

entry.title: Intercompany purchase for materials with account assignment or Lean services
entry.content:
Hi All

I'm in a greenfield implementation for S4Hana Cloud private.

In this project is required to implement an intercompany process starting in the purchase order but for materials with account assignment or Lean services (this is not possible in advanced intercompany).

For Cloud public edition this scenario exist: Scenario Billing of Costs Between Affiliated Companies (3ZB) but i can't find similar guide for Cloud private.

Can anyone share any implementation guide or documentation about this process?

entry.title: Re: Using two different versions of SAP B1 Clients on sam...
entry.content:
Hi Kbasher, 

The installation of two different B1 client patches on one machine is not supported. The installer will ask to uninstall the previous version as first.

Potentially you can copy and use client binaries from <SOFTWARE-PACKAGE>\Packages.x64\Client\Program Files 64\SAP\SAP Business One\SAP Business One.exe for test purposes.

Please note that the registration of libraries is not done in this option (in compared to installation) and can cause some issues for example in CR, DIAPI, Addons.

Best regards,
Darius

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

I have completed the necessary courses needed for ABAP cloud certification and sent mail as to digitalskills@sap.com with screenshot but yet to receive the reply. Can you please help on this.

Thank you,

Anagha Deshmukh

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account (SAP Universal ID) and getting started in the community
entry.content: Hello my name is Fatma, I am a woman from Egypt, and I am deeply interested in the SAP FI module as it plays a crucial role in streamlining financial processes and ensuring accurate reporting. I am eager to expand my knowledge and skills in this area to contribute to efficient financial operations.

entry.title: Re: IAS as central user management system
entry.content: You can configure multiple corporate IdPs with IAS. Additionally, you can integrate Azure and also use user management within IAS. This setup is particularly useful for exceptionbal cases, where a user does not exist in Azure but still needs access to your applications.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear David Chaviano,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Ali Furqan, and I am currently working with a Dubai-based organization that is in the process of implementing SAP ERP solutions. With over two years of experience in SAP MM consulting, I take pride in being part of a dedicated implementation team and contributing to the success of our projects.

Recently, I completed the course "Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procurement," which has deepened my understanding of SAP’s cutting-edge technology. I believe that obtaining professional certification is essential for my growth and for providing even greater value to my team and clients. Therefore, I am highly motivated to pursue certification to formalize my expertise and ensure I remain aligned with the latest industry standards.

In light of this, I would like to kindly request your support in securing a free certification attempt. I am confident that this opportunity will greatly enhance my skills and enable me to make a more significant contribution to my company’s ongoing and future SAP projects.

I sincerely appreciate your time and consideration, and I look forward to your response.

Best regards,
Ali Furqan

Can you help me in this ?

entry.title: Re: Phantom Indicator is selected in Material master and...
entry.content:
Hello Kamal, Could you please check validity BOM and Production version, I am assuming Production version may fall outside BOM validity date. To help you better, could you please share BOM and PV screenshot.

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Data into SAP HANA Cloud Using SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@DJSmith : At the moment it would push the data directly into HANA Cloud an dif you want to push the data to another HANA engine, you would need to selecta adifferent target connection, e.g. HANA Data lake Files in case you want to push the data into SAP HANA data lake files.
HANA Data Lake relational engine is currently not supported as target in replication flows.

Kind regards,

Daniel

entry.title: Calculated form rating & overall form rating should be separate for mid-year & year-end evaluations.
entry.content:
Customer Expectation- The customer conducts a mid-year performance cycle and aims to document the overall form rating separately for both mid-year and year-end reviews. Each KPI (goal) rating should be included in the summary section, along with the calculated form rating and overall ratings for both the mid-year and year-end evaluations.

Problem Statement- In the SuccessFactors PMGM module, there is only one standard Summary section, which means we cannot have separate calculated and overall form ratings for mid-year and year-end evaluations. Additionally, we are unable to provide distinct standard ratings for each KPI (goal) for both mid-year and year-end assessments.

Proposed Solution- To meet the requirements, we can maintain standard ratings for each KPI (goal) during the mid-year evaluation step. This will allow us to generate a calculated form rating in the summary section, along with a manually entered overall form rating. Each KPI rating will also be displayed in the summary. Below the summary section, we can create a custom section titled "Mid-Year Overall Rating," featuring two text boxes: 1) Mid-Year Calculated Rating and 2) Mid-Year Overall Rating. Users can enter the calculated rating in the first box and the overall rating in the second box to keep a record of both.

When the performance cycle progresses to year-end, users will see the same rating boxes where they entered the mid-year ratings for each KPI (goal), but in editable mode. They can overwrite the mid-year ratings with year-end ratings, which will generate a calculated rating for the year-end review. Users will also have the option to provide an overall form rating for year-end. The summary section will display the updated ratings provided by the user for each KPI (goal) at year-end. Additionally, the mid-year calculated and overall form ratings entered during the mid-year review will remain in the "Mid-Year Overall Rating" section for reference.

Is there anything else we can do to meet the customer requirement?

entry.title: Re: Number of patients how did the lab twice
entry.content: the formlua works with me, but the issue is that. When i try to count number of patients who did 2 labs, 0 has been shown. the used formula is =Count (How did 2 labs)

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Data into SAP HANA Cloud Using SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@qisu : When using HANA Cloud as target in Replication Flows, costs of Premium Outbound INtegration (=POI) dod not apply in this scenario and you only need to consider the data integration / execution hours as part of the SAP Datasphere sizing.
The pro's and cons depend also a little bit on the individual use case you are planning to implement, e.g. Replication Flows compared to HANA SDI, do not need any DP Agent that would need to be deployed & maintained by the customer and also offer additional target systems & source systems.

entry.title: Re: Excel connection to SAP failed after GUI 8.00 Update
entry.content: Hi, I am getting the same error. Since upgrading to SAP GUI 8 in 64bit (Office is on 64bit already) the macros are failing with above mentioned error message. I have alreay added wdtaocx.ocx to the references in VBA, but this has not solved the issue. Is there any update on how to fix this?

entry.title: Re: Setting "Reason&&&&" for Cancellation in Service Contract
entry.content:
HI,Kangkana！
Thank you for your answer.
Will it be possible to customize the reason for service contract cancellation reason in 2025?

Soma

very nice 360° (even roles covered!) blog about ALM defects and their feature. I tried to follow the link to the roadmap, but it is not specific. I tried to find the Roadmap Item addressing "direct connection between Defect and Feature objects", but may be I had tomates en los ojos, but I couldn't find it. Can you please share the title of the relating roadmap item?

Ciao Riccardo

entry.title: Re: Insert text type and content in notes tab in Freight...
entry.content: @MuditJain See my updated answer with a test report to create texts for a freight order

entry.title: Re: SAP and Arm64 version of Windows 11 with Prism emulator (Snapdragon X series)
entry.content: In regard to the ABAP Development Tools (ADT): Although a native Eclipse version is available, ADT is not natively supporting Windows on ARM. Using the PRISM translation layer, the x86_64 version of Eclipse incl. ADT should work but as you already mentioned there will be most likely an impact to the performance.

entry.title: Re: Wave Creation is not possible with Activity area cond...
entry.content: The storage bin and the activity area are not displayed in the warehouse request PDO , even the rough bin determination is activated in both WPT and ST

entry.title: test
entry.content: test

entry.title: "This application uses '@sap/cds' version 6.8, which is not compatible with the installed '@sap/cds-
entry.content:
Hi Experts , 

I am trying to run the CAPM applications from BAS to perform the changes and check it locally , but when click on run configuration and create run configuration I am not getting the child nodes to bind destination, xsuaa, and HANA DB.

Getting only play button next to run configuration instance ,if i click on that below error is coming. Please let me know if anyone faced same issue.Thanks 

This application uses '@sap/cds' version 6.8, which is not compatible with the installed '@sap/cds-dk' version 8

 

Thanks

Muraleedhar K

entry.title: Re: i had installed the vs code but when i try to access...
entry.content:
Application Wizard 1.6.7 has been released fixing compatibility with VSCode 1.94.x.
https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SAPOS.yeoman-ui

entry.title: Re: Prepack sales in store
entry.content:
Hi,

You cannot keep it as structured article, and also explode it to it's components parallel in the same store. 
If you need to sell them both way, and you are unable to get the components in the store individually, only as prepack (btw the example you have mentioned is more a sales set, not a prepack), then the only solution I see is to have the same product under two different article numbers as well, and make a special rule for one of them in the customizing of the structured articles by means of explosion. 

Best regards,
Andras

entry.title: Re: Assistance to setup Cloud identity services
entry.content:
Hello @piyushvns20,

setting up your corp IDP in IAS (Identity Providers -> Corporate Identity Providers) is a prerequisite.
Have you already finished this step?
Here's a how-to documentation (corp IDP is Entra) - step 6 "create corp idp": https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-ias-azure-ad.html.

Regards,
Christoph

entry.title: Combining Data from Multiple APIs in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi amazing SAP enthusiasts!

I hope you’re all having a great day! I’m diving into a project using SAP Build Apps and could really use your expertise on a particular challenge I’m facing.

I’m trying to display data in a single table that pulls from two different APIs. API1 gives me two of the fields I need, while API2 provides the other two. My goal is to combine these fields into one cohesive table.

If anyone has experience with this, I’d love to hear your tips or best practices! Are there specific functions or approaches within SAP Build Apps that can help me achieve this integration smoothly

Looking forward to your insights!

entry.title: Percentage Based Dynamic Sample Size
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I am faced with a complicated customer requirement. My searches on google have yielded no suitable result and I am posting here as a last resort. 

The Requirement: 

Stage 1 - 3 lots (Lot 1 to 3) inspected at 100% (Sample Size 100%)

Stage 2 - 3 lots (Lot 4 to 6) inspected at 50% (Sample Size 50%)

Stage 3 - 3 lots (Lot 7 to 9) inspected at 10% (Sample Size 10%)

Stage 4 - Any lots beyond this (Lot 10 and beyond) are skipped. 

Should any defect be found at this any stage, reset back to Stage 1. 

I believe the solution lay in a Dynamic Modification Rule (DMR) and a Sampling Scheme. However, there is no way to create a sampling scheme with a percentage-based sample size. 

Any suggestions on my course of action? 

Regards,

Dexter

entry.title: Re: How do you deal with User Custom Fields in Story Repo...
entry.content:
I completely agree, as Sunny says this is a showstopper for our company too in terms of moving to Story Reports for Learning (well, one of several showstoppers!).

I've just seen that another customer has submitted an influence request for this today:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/332338/?section=sectionDetails

Hopefully if this gets some traction then SAP will realise that the current solution isn't practical, and surely it would be easy enough to implement this change.

entry.title: Re: Prepack sales in store
entry.content: Thanks @ andras_strobl

entry.title: Re: SAC Live Data Connection Adding Measures
entry.content:
Hello,

if I remember correctly, it is not possible to add Calculations to a Live BW Model. You can however add Calculations to a story, that uses a Live BW Model. The Disadvantage is, that you can not reuse these Calculations in different stories, but the Functionality of creating additional measures should be available.

Let me know if this works for you.

Best Regards Adrian

entry.title: Re: VS Code SAP FIORI Tools - Application Modeler
entry.content: This was an issue with VS Code Update 1.94!

entry.title: Update custom form field via integration center
entry.content:
Hello, 

Does anyone know if it is possible to update a custom form field via the Integration Centre?

Thank you

Best regards

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Re: VS Code SAP FIORI Tools - Application Modeler
entry.content:
This was an issue with VS Code Update 1.94!

This is fixed with Application Version 1.93, but maybe you have to update the extension manually using "Install specific Version".

Regards Holger

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

Hi @David_Chaviano,

I would love to be part of the digital skills initiative and I am willing to learn more about SAP. I have already completed the SAP Learning Journey Discovering SAP Activate - Agile Project Delivery and I am keen to pursue the related certification. I feel that I am a good fit to the program because I identify as a person in the below categories:

- Women

- First generation college students

Thank you in advance for the opportunity!

Patrycja

entry.title: Re: Restoring Sap Hana cloud instance using a database backup file
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud recovery to another instance is on the roadmap for Q4/2024:
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800002881&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=000D3ABE772D1EED95CF5E8665C8E312
I have no idea whether this will be supported with a Trial Account. 
You may want to have a look into the HDI Container Export/Import functionality (though I do not know whether this works in a Trial Account):
Export/Import an SAP HDI Container for Copy Purpos... - SAP Community

entry.title: Store/DC as Manufacturing unit

Could you please help me to understand in Retail, can we create store as a manufacturing unit ?? if yes what are the required settings specific to Manufacturing store/DC that need to be adopted .

Thanks and Regards

Abhilash.E

entry.title: Data Migration Demystified: Legacy System Migration Workbench vs. SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit
entry.content:
What Is the Difference Between the Legacy Migration Workbench (LSMW) and the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit?

Legacy Migration Workbench (LSMW)

The LSMW is a tool that was initially built for migration from SAP R/2 to SAP R/3.  Then, as it was enhanced over the years, it came to be the data migration tool for SAP ERP. The user creates projects and subprojects. Within a subproject, the user creates a user-defined data migration object and selects a data migration method (BAPI (Business Application Programming Interfaces), IDoc (Intermediate Document), SAP program, batch input recording) to migrate the data. The target structure is either predefined (for SAP program), retrieved from the API (Application Programming Interface) structure (for IDoc, BAPI), or self-defined (for batch input recording).

  • The user needs to define the source structure themselves including fields and their length, description, and data types.
  • The source structure and source fields must then be assigned to the target structures and target fields and the complete value mapping must be done by the users themselves.
  • Next, the source data files must be defined and assigned to the structures, then the files need to be uploaded, and the data is converted into the target structures using the field and value mappings.

At the end, the user needs to trigger the upload. Depending on your data migration method, simulation is handled differently: it’s either provided in the method (SAP program) or, if possible, it must be implemented by the user (BAPI/IDocs), or it is not possible at all (batch input recording).  The LSMW is completely file based and allows users to upload files with a self-defined data structure.

The LSMW can be called an all-in-one data migration object modeling and execution workbench.

Technically, the LSMW is a backend transaction with transaction code LSMW.

 

SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit

The migration cockpit is a data migration tool built in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA including SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to migrate master data and transactional data. The user creates a migration project, selects data migration objects that are predefined by SAP and assigns them to that project. Technically, the predefined migration object definition is copied to the project.

The migration cockpit supports two different data migration approaches:

  1. Staging Tables: Data is stored in predefined temporary project- dependent staging tables that can be filled either directly on database level with the help of tools or with predefined migration template files in XML or CSV format. In the backend, the data migration is handled by the Migration Workbench (MWB).
  2. Direct Transfer: Data from four different scenarios can be directly extracted from SAP ERP, AFS, CRM (Customer Relationship Management), eSPP, EWM (Extended Warehouse Management) and then migrated into the respective SAP S/4HANA system (SAP S/4HANA, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition). For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition extraction from SAP ERP was released with 2402 (with 21 available migration objects). Here, the Posting Engine is the tool that handles the data migration in the backend.

Sender structure, target structure, structure mapping, field mapping, field and event rules are all predefined. After the source data is loaded into the staging tables or extracted from the SAP system, the user confirms the value mappings, then runs the simulation, and afterwards performs the data migration for the data migration objects.

Since the objects are predefined, the migration cockpit is a data migration execution tool.  All predefined data migration objects also support simulation. You can additionally monitor the data migration runs. The migration cockpit includes also functionality to analyze the return messages of the data simulation/migration.

The migration cockpit was initially based on the Web Dynpro backend transaction LTMC with limited functionality. The LTMC was retired with SAP S/4HANA 2021 and got replaced by the new SAP Fiori based Migrate Your Data app with better usability and much more functionality.

In addition, SAP delivers the SAP S/4HANA migration object modeler (LTMOM) that customers can use to model their own custom data migration objects, to modify the pre-delivered copied data migration objects, or to adapt the selection criteria (Direct Transfer approach). The LTMOM enhances the migration cockpit with modelling capabilities like those in the LSMW.

The LTMOM is a backend transaction and available for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with no restrictions.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the LTMOM is available as SAP Fiori app Model Your Migration Object (Harmonized UI (User Interface)) with limited functionality:

  • For a small number of migration objects, you can add other fields that are already available within the data migration API. Only fields for migration objects that were released by SAP with SAP Note 2999428 can be added.
  • You can adapt the extract selection criteria for the Direct Transfer approach.

The migration cockpit is neither designed to keep data in sync between different systems, nor to update existing data (for example, mass maintenance), nor to realize a continuous exchange or flow of data, nor to set up an integration scenario.

What Are the Main Differences Between LSMW and the Migration Cockpit?

Tool Definition

The LSMW is an all-in-one tool to create own migration objects and to run the data migration. The LSMW has no analyzing or monitoring capabilities. A simulation of the data migration is not foreseen and for some migration methods (batch input) is not even possible. Simulation must be implemented manually in case the API supports the simulation mode.

The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit is split into two tools:

  1. the migration cockpit to simulate, perform, monitor, and analyze a data migration run
  2. the migration object modeler to modify existing and create user-defined data migration objects.

Supported Data Sources

The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit and the LSMW support CSV files as data source. The migration cockpit also supports Microsoft Excel's specific XML Spreadsheet 2003 file format. The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit approach Direct Transfer can even retrieve data directly from selected SAP source systems.

 

Legacy System Migration Workbench

SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit

CSV files

X

X

XML files

-

X

Direct transfer from SAP source systems

-

X

Number of Delivered Migration Objects

For the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit, the predefined data migration objects include:

  • API call definition, with simulation handling
  • sender structure definition, with
    • the source structure/extraction model,
    • source fields, key definition,
    • view definition for structure and field visibility
  • the structure mapping to the data migration API
  • the field mapping
  • predefined value mapping and event rules
  • integration into Data Migration Status app
  • key mapping to jump to validation app for the migrated instances
  • calculated view and calculated availability to show objects, structures, and fields based on activated countries and licenses

 

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

Staging Tables Approach

228

277

Direct Transfer Approach

259

21

For the LSMW, a list of ~66 usable SAP migration programs (table SXDA0) with predefined target structures were delivered. Though the list of BAPIs and IDocs seems long, not all of them can be used within LSMW. All data migration objects must be created on your own including source structure, source fields, structure relations, field mapping and conversion rules, translations, file definition and file to target structure mapping.

Supported Migration Methods

The LSMW supports the following migration methods:

  • Predefined SAP Standard Migration reports
  • Backend transaction recording and then use this as batch input data migration method
  • BAPI with BAPI/ALE interface and related IDoc structure and IDoc type.
    • BAPIs without IDoc structure can only be used once the IDoc structure and type will be generated via transaction BDBG
  • IDoc

The SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit supports the following migration methods:

  • Function module calls (including BAPI) with specific compatibility restrictions (see SAP Note 2590165 and SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit - Creating Your Own Function Modules in LTMOM)
  • CDS (Core Data Services) Behavior Definition (RAP/BO) with specific compatibility restrictions for example a CREATE operation on the root node

 

Legacy System Migration Workbench

SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit + SAP S/4HANA Migration Object Modeler

BAPI

supported if BAPI has BAPI/ALE interface and related IDoc structure and IDoc type

supported with some restrictions on test run flags and Commit/Rollback handling (restrictions see SAP Note 2590165)

Function modules

not supported

supported (restrictions see SAP Note 2590165)

CDS behavior definition (RAP/BO)

not supported

supported with some restrictions

IDoc

supported

not supported

Own transaction recordings

supported

not supported

Special SAP Migration Programs

supported if migration object, program and target structure is predefined within the SXDA tables

not supported

 

 

Why Does the Migration Cockpit Not Support the Change of Existing Business Objects?

First of all, the data is not stored directly in the business application tables, neither with the LSMW nor the migration cockpit.
The migration cockpit, like the LSMW, calls an API with a defined target structure to migrate the data into the SAP S/4HANA system.
The APIs used by the migration objects (normally) only support the CREATE method.
It is therefore not the migration cockpit that limits the capability to make a change, it is the API, used within the data migration object, that doesn't support the UPDATE.

In LSMW, it depends on the selected migration method of the business object: If the method doesn't support the update mode, you cannot update the data of the business object as well.

With the migration object modeler, same as in LSMW, the user can create a user-defined data migration object using either a supported API, delivered by SAP or created by themselves to UPDATE data.

Second, the migration cockpit stores the success of a data migration run for a specific migration instance within a data migration project related database table (restart table). Therefore, a successfully migrated migration object instance cannot be migrated again with the same migration project.

    • Even if the API supported the UPDATE of a business object, you would need to create a new migration project to migrate the same instance.

Third, for specific scenarios, for example, phased-rollout, special EXTEND migration objects are delivered, for example Customer - extend existing record by multiple addresses. With such an EXTEND migration object, you can migrate data for new organization levels (company code, plant, ...) or other structures (addresses, long text). Objects that you can extend with new company code data, are, for example, Customer, Supplier, Product, or G/L account.

To update existing business objects, SAP recommends using existing mass maintenance apps for specific business objects. These mass maintenance apps are built to update or make recurring changes on business objects.

Can I Still Use LSMW to Update Business Objects?

Sure. It is not forbidden to use the LSMW in SAP S/4HANA. In SAP Note 2287723 it is clearly said that "...the use of LSMW is restricted in the context of data migrations to SAP S/4HANA...". Restricted does not mean forbidden.

The fact that the LSMW is on the Simplification List  means that using the tool is not recommended and at the user's own responsibility. Esp. as there is no further support or new functionality released for this tool.

 If you want to create a new migration object or update existing data and a transaction or a BAPI/IDoc that supports the create or update method exists and can be used in SAP S/4HANA, you can still use the LSMW.  But as user, you must first check if the data migration method, you want to use, has any restrictions for SAP S/4HANA. 

It is also your choice to use the LTMOM to create your own data migration objects using change-APIs.
In both tools, you always need to create such user-defined migration objects on your own. 

The effort is the same with LTMOM as with LSMW.

See Also

2287723  - LSMW in SAP S/4HANA on-premise (S4CORE) - SAP for Me

2733253 - FAQ for SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit. Migration approach: Transfer / Migrate data using staging tables - SAP for Me 

2684818  - SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit usage for mass processing data or as interface

2590165  - SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit - Creating Your own Function Modules

2481235  - Restrictions and extensibility of pre-delivered migration objects - SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit (on premise)

entry.title: Re: 🔵 How to build a GenAI application using SAP BTP, Generative AI Hub and Amazon Bedrock
entry.content:
In this Video Raj and Abhi walked us through a use case where we can consume a pretrained model, deploy it and then give it access to new data and then prompt the llm on that data. But what if a use case would require us to retrain a model on a specific behavior. Lets say it doesnt understand how our provided Datasets are related and the llm makes mistakes because of that. 
Is there a way to train a foundation model on new data ? Could i for example write a prompting script which explains the relation between datasets before allowing users to prompt the llm ? Or what would be the best pracitce way to adjust to mistakes an llm could make ?

Btw. great presentation, Raj and Abhi did an amazing job walking us through all the steps to set everything up and get started !

entry.title: Re: SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @FeliciaB 
Welcome to our SAP Learning Group and thanks for your engagement.
I hope others may weigh-in with their own ideas
Thanks to @Andrew who chimed in on this already.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere – New Replication Flow
entry.content:
Hello @arvshar2, that is not supported as a source in a replication flow at the moment. You could use a hyperscaler's object store like Amazon S3, Azure Data lake or Google Cloud Storage. You could also use a dataflow to move data into Datasphere using Generic SFTP, and then use the Replication Flow to replicate to one of the supported targets.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: CPI: API has restriction to pull only 100 records in...
entry.content:
@naveenkk 

Please use a looping process to call the respective service, retrieve the data, and append it to a variable using the content modifier until the loop exits. Once the loop is complete, return the appended data into the main flow body using the content modifier.

entry.title: SAP TM Charge calculation. Calculating charge items based on other charge items
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I would like to ask if there is a functionality based on Charge Calculation under TM on which calculation of charge items will be dependent on other charges entered.

For example:

The special rate here is part of the root.

The 2 rates below are initially dependent on the Special Rate 

10% of Special rate will be under Fuel

16.63% of Special rate will be under Management Fee.

If I want to have the Management Fee rate to be dependent aside from the Special Rate (root), is to have the Fuel charge value to be added and multiply it based on the percentage based on the rate table. 

Would this be possible on computing it on the FA alone or any config?

Or this would be an enhancement to consider

Thanks alot

Rayomi

entry.title: Re: Can't open Template Wizard
entry.content:
Hi,

To resolve this issue, please, update to Application Wizard 1.16.7, it includes a fix that make it compatible to VSCode 1.94.0.

Regards,

Rima

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Advanced Formula "...
entry.content:
@William_Yu and @N1kh1l 

I did some further investigation and discovered that the Data Action works only when there are 6 or fewer dimensions in the model. It doesn't seem to matter which specific dimensions are included, as long as the total number doesn't exceed six. I'm also working with other tenants, and this limitation seems quite odd. I've used this formula in models with around 15 dimensions without any issues.

Do you have any thoughts on this?

entry.title: drop down list showing "restriction"
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

I am doing testing for S/4 HANA FI and all the list drop-downs are showing restrictions.

Can someone guide me as to why is this happening and how to solve this please.

Thanks.

entry.title: How to mass deploy iFlows for Service Cloud Utilities Add-on
entry.content:
Introduction

With the growing number of iFlows that need to be deployed, the iFlows for the SAP Service Cloud Version 2, Utilities add-on is designed to support a simple deployment. Here is a short description of the concept and a link to a video where the process of the deployment is shown.

Disclaimer: This should not be considered a best practice for production, since this was done for a demo system.

 

How to deploy iFlows

To enable this mass deployment the iFlows is designed to read the parameters needed from local storage in SAP Integration Suite. One special iFlow is delivered that will populate this storage.

For the SAP Service Cloud Version 2, Utilities add-on 3 iFlow packages are delivered:

  • SAP Service Cloud SAP Service Cloud Version 2, Utilities add-on for Utilities Master Data Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Service Cloud SAP Service Cloud Version 2, Utilities add-on for Utilities Contract Account Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Service Cloud SAP Service Cloud Version 2, Utilities add-on for Utilities Premise Integration with SAP S/4HANA

 

The mass deployment is only relevant for package for Contract Account and Premise, not the master data replication iFlow.

Link to help document:  Configuration in SAP Cloud Integration | SAP Help Portal

 

The steps to deploy the iFlows are illustrated below.

 

Before starting the process pieces of information need to be collected:

  1. The host name and port for the S/4HANA system. When using the SAP Cloud Connector, this will be the ‘virtual hostname’ and not the actual hostname of the S/4HANA system.
  2. The client ID for the S/4HANA system
  3. Username and password for the technical user used for communication between SAP Integration Suite and S/4HANA.

With the above data available the configuration can start.

This is a link to a video showing the steps below in the system.

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Create User credentials in SAP Integration Suite.
    1. Here it is mandatory to create a user credential with the name ‘S4-COMM-USER’ as this is hard coded into the iFlows.
  2. Configure the Automated Integration iFlow
    1. Update with S/4HANA host name and port.
    2. The ‘Cloud Connector Location ID’ is often left blank if there is only one Cloud Connector in the environment.  In all cases this field must have the same value as the Location ID in the Cloud Connector. If it is left blank in the Cloud Connector, then just delete the entry in the field here.
    3. Update the client id.
  3. Deploy the Automated Integration iFlow
    1. The deployment will trigger the creation of 4 variables in the SAP Integration Suite data storage.
    2. The data in local storage will be available for 400 days. If the iFlow is undeployed and then re-deployed the variable will be created again.
    3. Another option is to edit this iFlow and configure the timer step to execute the iFlow automatically once a year.
  4. Deploy all iFlows from the Contract Account package.
    1. All iFlows in the package, EXCEPT for the Automated Integration iFlow and go to ‘configure.’
    2. In the configuration screen there is no need for additional configuration, just select the ‘deploy all’.
    3. Repeat this for the Premise package. Here all iFlows can be selected and then deployed.
  5. All the iFlows will now at run time read the configuration from the local storage in SAP Integration Suite and be ready for use.

 

Conclusion

With the above procedure, we have deployed more than 60 iFlows in less than 10 minutes.

As mentioned above – keep in mind the data in local storage is only retained for 400 days. This need to be part of the scheduled maintenance for updates.

If you have comments or further questions to the above, please use the comment field and I will try and answer them.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano,

I hope this message finds you well.

I would like to introduce myself as an individual who aligns with the following categories:

  1. Residents of remote or underserved areas who may lack access to the same economic opportunities as those in urban or more affluent regions.
  2. People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I am eager to enhance my skills and expand my knowledge in pursuit of new opportunities.

Thank you for your time.

Best regards,
Talha Naveed

entry.title: Re: Sync & Use SWIFT Code (Bank Data) from SAP ECC to SAP...
entry.content:
Dear Smeta,

the concept for synchronizing bank account information does not exist. Therefore a custom logic needs to be implemented.

SWIFT Code is needed to identity the bank account master data in ERP backend system in order to store the bank account information of a supplier. 

We now go for transmitting the SWIFT code in the XML payload from ARIBA-SLP to ERP backend system with supplier field mapping vendor.bankInfos.bankAccountExternalID.

Then within inbound processing we use the SWIFT code in order to find the bank account master data for the reletated bank account information. Then we deleted the SWIFT code out of the field External ID. So that it will not be stored within this field in SAP ECC. The SWIFT Code comes then from the bank master data.

Kind regards,

Tobias

entry.title: Re: Visual Modeler SAP Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
Hi Lucia,

if the graphical modeler is inside CPE you can it just how you are using it in PaPM Cloud, this I know you are aware how to 🙂 but for SCT I would recommend to wait for SCT experts to provide their inputs.

But for PaPM Cloud there are App Help or blogposts in our community that you can search for.

Best!
Paulo M.

entry.title: Performance Appraisal MSS POWL Query and Tab sequence
entry.content:
In the performance appraisal POWL, we need to add a specific tab that will exclusively display the appraisals pending for action with the user who is currently logged in. This will help the user easily identify and focus on the appraisals that require their attention.

 

So depending upon the above requirement we have created the Custom query “ZOSA_POWL_ESS_PENDING_QUERY”. In t-code POWL_QUERYR we have assigned the sequence of query as 1, the tab is moved on position 1 but still focus is on standard tab when the page is opened.

 

How to change the focus in powl query to first tab when the page is open for first time.

entry.title: Re: Database restore / Cleaning activity for SAP S4HANA P...
entry.content:
Dear George,

Thanks for your valuable input.

Regards,

Nitin

entry.title: Re: Distribution channel status
entry.content: Hi EXPERTS! Can anyone provide feedback on the above question please? I am trying to create the exact same thing. Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP PaPM Cloud: Remote Source Properties
entry.content:
Hi Riccardo,

Unfortunately, it is not possible to display more than the current limit of HANA tables as remote objects in a schema. The system sets the limitation, and there are no remote source settings that can increase the number of displayed tables. However, you can use the search function to find the specific table you need.

Best regards,

Milan

entry.title: Inquiry on Rounding Tax Field in AR Invoice (SAP Business One)
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I would like to inquire if there is any possibility of rounding the Tax field in the AR invoice window in SAP Business One. Currently, the system uses 3 decimal places, but my aim is to round this to 2 decimal places.

Could you please advise on whether this adjustment is possible, and if so, the steps required to implement it? If you need any further clarification, please feel free to let me know.

entry.title: Re: IAS as central user management system
entry.content:
Hello @BhagyaVenkatesha,

Thank you very much for your response.

For me, the following is still unclear. For example, @Colt  (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-public-cloud-access-using-ias-as-proxy-but-no-user-in-the-identity/qaq-p/13734888) points out that users should be persisted in the SAP IAS User Store.

I don’t understand the purpose of keeping users in the IAS User Store. For example, if I provision users from Azure to IAS, I still have to manage the users in Azure (like changing users’ names, emails, etc., deleting, and adjusting). If I change a user in IAS , those changes will be overwritten by the next IPS job Or if I delete a user in IAS, that user will be provisioned again from Azure to IAS in the next IPS job.

So what is the point of persisting users in IAS if I still have to manage the users in Azure (except, of course, for the apps that require users to be in IAS)?

Thank you very much !!!

entry.title: Re: Invalid times for dummy activities
entry.content:
I also faced a similar issue. For PO i received this error <b>"Invalid times for dummy activities".</b>  in CPP. The root cause was PIR was not CIFed to ePPDS. After doing RIMODINI for the integration model, it got resolved.

 

Regards

Yogesh Kumar

entry.title: Re: How to link new bank account to an entity in SAP BYD?
entry.content:
Hello Sameer,

After you linked the bank g/l account to an Account Determination Group for a specific Chart of Accounts and Account Determination Profile - you should select this Account Determination Group (ADG) for the selected House-Bank Account under:

WoC: Liquidity Management / Master Data / My Bank ID / Edit Bank Accounts => Account /
Account Determination Group.

Hope this helps,

Frank

entry.title: Re: SAP COURSE
entry.content:
Hi @MusscaN 
I recommend to access the student zone of SAP Learning to start learning and check out some of the career opportunities!
Regarding  learning material I recommend to access our learning journeys posted on our SAP Learning platform  and check our  Learning Journeys. These are interactive, visual maps illustrating recommended paths to SAP software competency, or expansion of skill levels for all roles and SAP solutions. They provide easy-to-navigate, learning content to help you gain the skills you need and prepare for an SAP Certification. You can follow the recommendations and track your progress toward your learning goals. Or access a topic related course. You can browse all courses. If you you filter for SAP ERP you see a great variety:  here
Are you looking for SAP ERP Asset Management, SAP ERP Finance, SAP ERP Logistic Executions and Warehouse Management, SAP ERP Product Management or else?

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: How to check change log for condition contract
entry.content: Thanks @Gauthier Victor . I will implement the note and check the behavior and confirm back.

entry.title: Re: Cors issue when using API
entry.content:
@amelendez Hi Cors issue is not solved by using the ui5 code snippet given by Api Hub. My advice is just to refer to the DMC Custom Extensions Github package. 

Br,

Emre Yoldas

entry.title: How to insert URL in SO10 standard text in sap for SAP script print preview in sales order (VA03)?
entry.content:
Hi Kudos,

Greetings of the day!
I am trying to get the URL/HYPER link in sales order print preview from SO1O text. I am using the SO10 text in SAP Script.

I tried several methods href, SO72....etc t-code but I am not able to get the hyper/URL link it is printing as hard code text in the print preview hyper link.

can some one help here!

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Business Technology Platform with Blockchain Service
entry.content:
now it is possible:

 

Running Your Own Blockchain on The SAP BTP Kyma Trial: A Hands On How To Guide 🚀
